events.headline: Gilded Age: Victorian Propriety Enforced by the Pew
events.body: The rules of polite Gilded Age society are explicitly Christian in form: no Sabbath commerce, no mixed bathing, no theater on Sunday, no unseemly display by women. The church backs the courthouse; the minister is also the magistrate of community reputation. In this world that alignment never breaks — the informal moral order of the Victorian Protestant establishment becomes the template for codified theocratic law a century later.

events.headline: The Comstock Act: Obscenity as Federal Morality Law
events.body: Anthony Comstock's postal censorship law bans 'obscene, lewd, or lascivious' materials from the mail — including contraception information. Rather than being progressively weakened as in the default timeline, in this world the Comstock framework is reinforced decade by decade by evangelical lobbying. The precedent that federal law may enforce theological standards of decency is never successfully challenged.

events.headline: WCTU: Women's Moral Authority Is Church Authority
events.body: Frances Willard's Women's Christian Temperance Union is the largest women's organization in America and its moral code is explicitly evangelical. In the default timeline, the WCTU's influence wanes after Prohibition's repeal. Here, Prohibition never fully fails: in thirty-two states it remains legally intact, and the WCTU evolves into the 'Women's Covenant Society' — the primary enforcer of community moral standards and later a constituent body of the Council of Elders.

events.headline: The Scopes Verdict Holds: Evolution Leaves the Curriculum Permanently
events.body: John Scopes is convicted, and in this world the conviction sticks in thirty-one states. 'Creation science' — the proposition that Genesis is accurate natural history — becomes the required framework for biology instruction. A generation of students graduates understanding the age of the earth to be roughly six thousand years. Their relationship with empirical science is permanently altered, and the rift between faith communities and scientific institutions calcifies.

events.headline: Divorce Criminalized in Eleven States: The Covenant Marriage
events.body: Under pressure from evangelical lobbyists, eleven states pass 'Covenant Marriage' laws that make divorce legally nearly impossible without evidence of adultery or abandonment. Unlike the optional covenant marriage laws of the 1990s in the default timeline, these are mandatory — no alternative. Domestic violence advocates warn that the laws trap women. The response from pulpits is that submission is scriptural.

events.headline: Blue Laws Reinforced: The Sabbath Is Federal Policy
events.body: The 'Lord's Day Act' passes the Senate with evangelical coalition support, making Sunday commerce a federal misdemeanor except for essential services. Rather than the progressive erosion that blue laws suffered in the default timeline, here they are strengthened. By 1960, American commercial culture has a mandatory weekly pause that its trading partners find economically baffling. The pause is not restful — it is enforced.

events.headline: Griswold v. Connecticut Goes the Other Way: Contraception Remains Illegal
events.body: In the default timeline the Supreme Court finds a constitutional right to contraception. Here, a Dominionist-majority court upholds Connecticut's contraception ban as a valid exercise of the state's interest in 'encouraging natural family formation.' The ruling reverberates across twenty-three states which immediately pass their own bans. Women's access to reproductive planning is now a function of denomination and geography.

events.headline: Anita Bryant Wins: Sodomy Laws Nationalized After Amendment 2
events.body: Anita Bryant's 'Save Our Children' campaign succeeds not just in Dade County but ignites a national amendment movement. A constitutional amendment classifying homosexual conduct as contrary to the 'moral foundations of the Republic' passes thirty-two states in 1978 — the same year the Moral Majority is founded to capitalize on the momentum. The LGBTQ rights movement is driven fully underground. Sodomy laws are enforced nationally.

events.headline: The Human Life Amendment: Abortion Abolished by Scripture
events.body: The Moral Majority's first legislative priority — a constitutional amendment declaring personhood to begin at fertilization — passes after Reagan's landslide provides the Senate margins. Unlike Roe's reversal in the default timeline, this is not a return to state regulation but a federal abolition grounded explicitly in Exodus 21. All abortion is murder under federal law. The amendment's preamble quotes Scripture. Enforcement begins immediately.

events.headline: The Family Protection Act: Gender Roles Encoded in Federal Law
events.body: The Council of Christian Citizens succeeds in passing the 'Family Protection Act,' which defines the family as a husband, a wife, and their natural children; bars federal funding for any entity that 'challenges traditional sex roles'; and mandates that married women's tax filings be subordinate to their husband's. Feminism is not illegal, but its institutional infrastructure is defunded.

events.headline: The Covenant Communications Board: Scripture as Content Standard
events.body: The 'Decency in Broadcasting Act' establishes the Covenant Communications Board to replace the FCC, staffed by clergy and charged with evaluating all broadcast content against a scriptural standard. Network television loses thirty percent of its programming inventory in the first year of reviews. Hollywood relocates its morally controversial productions to secular offshore studios. The American cultural economy splits: sanitized content for the domestic market, everything else exported.

events.headline: The Digital Sabbath Mandate: Internet Shuts Down on Sundays
events.body: The Council of Elders extends the Lord's Day Act to digital networks. Internet service providers are required to block non-essential traffic on Sundays from midnight to midnight. Scripture-approved content — church broadcasts, Bible study apps, approved hymn services — remains accessible. Social media, entertainment streaming, and secular news are gated. The measure is justified as 'protecting families from the temptations of the digital bazaar.'

events.headline: The Covenant Curriculum: All Public Education Must Center Scripture
events.body: The Ministry of Covenant Education, replacing the Department of Education, mandates that Scripture be the integrating framework for every subject. Mathematics classes open with prayer; history is taught as providential narrative; literature must feature only morally improving texts approved by the Heresy Tribunal. Teachers must hold a Certificate of Doctrinal Soundness. The secular private school sector grows rapidly among those who can afford it, creating a two-tier cognitive class.

events.headline: The Modesty Statute: Dress Codes Become Federal Regulation
events.body: The Council of Elders, responding to a pastoral commission report on 'immodesty as a vector of social decay,' passes the Modesty Statute. Women must cover shoulders, knees, and hair in public spaces. Men must not go shirtless in any public area outside designated athletic venues. Violations carry a first-offense fine and mandatory scriptural counseling. Fashion industry compliance officers become a growth profession.

events.headline: The Tongue of Zion: Secular Language Regulated by the Office of Doctrinal Integrity
events.body: The Office of Doctrinal Integrity issues its 'Sanctified Language Guidelines,' prohibiting profanity, blasphemy, and any public speech that 'promotes doctrinal error or undermines the Covenant order.' 'Goddamn' is a fifty-dollar fine; 'God doesn't exist' said publicly is a misdemeanor. The Guidelines also regulate which pronouns are 'covenant-consistent.' Linguists in secular states study the Anointed Republic's sociolinguistics as a case study in imposed sacred register.

events.headline: Moral Credit Score: Citizens Rated by Tithing, Church Attendance, and Confession Records
events.body: The Ministry of Covenant Life introduces the 'Sanctification Index' — a citizen scoring system that combines tithe payment records, verified church attendance (via QR-code check-in systems), and pastoral evaluation reports. High scorers receive preference in housing, employment, and education. Low scorers are flagged for 'covenant restoration counseling.' The system is described by the Supreme Pastor as 'helping citizens walk in accountability.' Critics who survive note it is the most comprehensive surveillance system in American history.

events.headline: The Purity of Faith Act: Interfaith Marriage Requires Pastoral Dispensation
events.body: The Purity of Faith Act prohibits civil marriage between members of different designated faith groups without a pastoral dispensation from both parties' clergy. Marriages between Covenant Christians and 'sojourners' (non-Christians) are automatically void. The act is nominally about protecting children's religious identity; it functions as a mechanism for enforcing endogamy within the Covenant community and isolating non-Christian populations from full civic participation.

events.headline: New Zion's Children Develop Looser Mores: The Martian Generation Rebels
events.body: The first generation born entirely on Mars has never known Earth's Covenant enforcement apparatus. Harsh survival conditions have forced pragmatic rule-breaking: women lead engineering teams, couples partner outside their denomination for warmth and practical support, the Sabbath Internet shutdown is impossible to enforce across interplanetary delay. When Earth broadcasts its horror at Martian moral laxity, the Martian settlers point out that the Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath. The Martian Schism's theological divergence begins in the mess halls, not the chapels.

events.headline: The Sanctified Society at Its Most Complete: Covenant Mores Blanket Public Life
events.body: By the end of the twenty-first century the Anointed Republic's public life is the most comprehensively morality-regulated society in the post-Industrial world. Alcohol is illegal, the Sabbath is enforced digitally and physically, clothing is regulated, language is monitored, marriage is a theological contract, and the entire educational system produces citizens who know the Bible better than any other text. From within, it is described as the most righteous nation in human history. From without, it is indistinguishable from the systems its founders claimed to oppose.
